section_miscategorizations.text: He also reports acne on his face and uses Persa-gel.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The phrase 'uses Persa-gel' describes a current medication the patient is already taking, which belongs in S, but in the context of the overall note it conflicts with the Plan statement 'Start Persa-gel daily,' implying it is both an existing and a new medication. This is an organizational/consistency issue rather than a pure section misplacement.

As a Plan item this is correctly placed in P, but it contradicts the S section statement that the patient already uses Persa-gel, which could mislead a reader about whether this is a new prescription or continuation. The section is correct; the inconsistency creates a minor documentation quality issue.
